title: TUI: Highlight current match and add n/p navigation
description:
In normal mode, show which match is current and allow n/p to navigate through matches.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Track current match index (already have searchMatchIdx)
- Highlight current match differently from other matches
- In normal mode, 'n' jumps to next match, 'p' to previous
- Keep search query active when exiting search mode (already works)
- Show current match position in status (e.g., "3 of 15")

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Change highlighting: current match = bold/inverted, other matches = dim
- Add 'n' and 'p' handlers in updateNormalMode for match navigation
- Update help text to show n/p for navigation

NOTES:
- Search query persists when exiting search mode (already implemented)
- Matches are highlighted when not in search mode (already implemented)
- Need to distinguish current match highlight from other matches
close_reason: Implemented: n/p match navigation with auto-scroll, current match highlighting (bold/inverted), Enter jumps to first match, match counter in status bar. Full test coverage.

title: TUI: Add text search
description:
Add incremental search within current log view.

REQUIREMENTS:
- / or Ctrl+F to enter search mode  
- Type search term (case-insensitive)
- Highlight all matches in current view
- n/N for next/previous match
- ESC to exit search and clear highlights
- Search within scrollable content (depends on scroll implementation)
- Search remains active across all views (navigate to another log source with arrow keys, search still applies)

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add search state to model (query, current match index)
- Add search mode to Update() for key handling
- Update renderLogs() to highlight matching text
- Navigate matches with scroll position
- Persist search query when switching views

NICE-TO-HAVE:
- Show match count (e.g., "3 of 15 matches")
- Regex support toggle
- Case-sensitive toggle

ACCEPTANCE:
- User can find text in logs quickly
- Matches are clearly highlighted
- Navigation between matches is intuitive
- Works with scrolling
- Search persists when switching between log sources
close_reason: Implemented text search with cross-view persistence

title: Implement stop_all_processes MCP tool
description:
Implement stop_all_processes MCP tool that allows AI agents to stop all managed processes. Tool should:
- Call manager.Stop() method
- Return count of processes stopped
- Handle manager unavailable case
- Provide confirmation message with process names

This wraps the existing POST /processes/stop-all endpoint logic.

title: Add authentication to MCP endpoint
description:
Secure the /mcp endpoint with authentication to prevent unauthorized access.

Current state: /mcp endpoint is completely open, allowing any client to query logs and manage processes.

Options to consider:
- API key/token authentication (simplest)
- IP whitelist for local development
- OAuth/JWT for production use
- Configuration flag to enable/disable auth

Recommended approach:
- Add optional API token via config/env var
- Check Authorization header in MCP handler
- Return 401 Unauthorized if token missing/invalid
- Document in agent integration guide

Also consider:
- Rate limiting per client
- Request timeout and size limits
- Audit logging of MCP requests

Note: CORS is intentionally permissive for development. Tighten in production deployments.
close_reason: Not needed - REST API on :9000 is already open for local development. MCP tools wrap the same endpoints, adding authentication would be security theater without real benefit for a local dev tool.

title: Phase 2.5: Quality of Life & Bug Fixes
description:
Polish the foundation before building agent features. Make Running Man daily-driver ready.

Success criteria: TUI usable for daily work, no showstopper bugs, logs are readable and accessible
close_reason: All Phase 2.5 tasks complete: TUI match navigation with n/p, cursor movement in search, log level search working

title: Wire up MCP protocol handling
description:
Connect MCP server to HTTP handler properly using StreamableServerTransport.

Current state: createMCPHandler() creates an mcp.Server but returns placeholder JSON instead of using the server.

Required changes:
- Use StreamableServerTransport with session management
- Wire transport.ServeHTTP() to handle MCP protocol requests
- Handle GET (SSE stream) and POST (messages) requests
- Remove placeholder JSON response

This makes the /mcp endpoint functional at the protocol level, ready for tool implementations to work.

References:
- go doc mcp.StreamableServerTransport
- SDK examples for HTTP integration
close_reason: Wired up MCP protocol handling using SDK's NewStreamableHTTPHandler(). The endpoint now properly handles GET (SSE), POST (messages), and DELETE (session termination). Added comprehensive documentation. Ready for tool implementations.

title: Update CORS middleware for MCP headers
description:
Update corsMiddleware() in server.go:
- Add MCP-Protocol-Version to allowed headers
- Add MCP-Session-Id to allowed headers
- Ensure OPTIONS preflight works correctly

Required for MCP protocol compliance
close_reason: Updated CORS middleware with MCP protocol headers. Added Mcp-Session-Id, Mcp-Protocol-Version, Last-Event-ID to allowed headers. Added DELETE method. Added preflight caching and inline documentation.

title: Create basic TUI command structure

This provides more detail than get_process_status which shows all processes.

title: Test MCP endpoint with OpenCode
description:
Manual testing steps for MCP endpoint integration with OpenCode:

## TESTING STATUS: READY FOR FINAL VERIFICATION AFTER OPENCODE RESTART

### Critical Bug Found and Fixed:
1. **BUG**: Server binary was outdated, only registering 4/8 MCP tools
2. **ROOT CAUSE**: Binary built before all tools were implemented
3. **FIX**: Rebuilt binary with latest code (Mar 1 19:20+)
4. **VERIFICATION**: Server now registers ALL 8 tools (see logs)

### Current State (Server Side):
- ✅ Server running on :9000 with rebuilt binary
- ✅ ALL 8 MCP tools registered in server logs:
  1. search_logs
  2. get_recent_errors
  3. get_process_status
  4. get_startup_logs
  5. restart_process
  6. stop_all_processes
  7. get_process_detail (additional tool)
  8. get_health_status
- ✅ 4 core tools previously tested and working
- ✅ Underlying HTTP endpoints for remaining tools verified

### Blocking Issue:
- ❌ OpenCode caching previous tool discovery (only shows 4 tools)
- ✅ **Solution**: Restart OpenCode session to force re-discovery

### Testing Completed (BEFORE fix):
#### ✅ search_logs Tool Tests - ALL PASSED
- "Show recent errors from the last 5 minutes" - Works
- "Search logs for 'database' in llamaindex-pg process" - Works
- "Get info logs from running-man in the last hour" - Works
- "Find logs containing 'Address already in use'" - Works

#### ✅ get_recent_errors Tool Tests - ALL PASSED
- "Show me recent errors with stacktraces" - Works with context
- "Get errors from llamaindex-pg process with context" - Works
- "Check for errors in backend process" - Works

#### ✅ get_process_status Tool Tests - ALL PASSED
- "What processes are running-man managing?" - Works
- "Check status of the backend process" - Works
- "Show all managed processes" - Works

#### ✅ get_startup_logs Tool Tests - ALL PASSED
- "Show me startup logs for the backend" - Works
- "Get initial logs when llamaindex-pg started" - Works

### Remaining Tests (AFTER OpenCode restart):
#### Tools to verify once OpenCode exposes them:
1. **get_health_status** - Test: "Check running-man system health"
2. **get_process_detail** - Test: "Get detailed info about backend process"
3. **restart_process** - Test error handling only: "Restart non-existent process" (safe)
4. **stop_all_processes** - Test error handling only: "Stop all processes without confirm flag" (safe)

### Verification Steps After Restart:
1. **Tool Discovery**: Verify OpenCode shows 8 tools (not 4)
2. **New Tool Testing**: Test get_health_status and get_process_detail
3. **Safety Testing**: Test error handling for destructive tools (don't actually restart/stop)
4. **Documentation**: Verify tool descriptions match functionality

### Success Criteria (Updated):
- ✅ Server registers all 8 tools (verified)
- ✅ 4/8 tools tested and working (verified)
- ⏳ 4/8 tools need testing after OpenCode re-discovery
- ✅ Output is readable and helpful for debugging
- ✅ Error handling works correctly
- ✅ Documentation is clear and complete

### Key Findings:
1. Testing uncovered critical deployment bug
2. MCP implementation actually has 8 tools (not 7 as planned)
3. OpenCode caches MCP tool discovery - requires restart
4. Destructive tools (restart_process, stop_all_processes) should only be tested for error handling

### Next Action Required:
**RESTART OPENCODE SESSION** to pick up new MCP tool configuration, then complete remaining tests.

### Notes for Next Tester:
- Server is running with rebuilt binary (Mar 1 19:20+)
- All 8 tools registered in server logs
- OpenCode needs fresh session to discover new tools
- Test destructive tools ONLY for error handling (use invalid process names or missing confirm flag)
close_reason: All 8 MCP tools tested and working. Server rebuilt with latest binary, OpenCode session restarted successfully. All tests passed: search_logs, get_recent_errors, get_process_status, get_startup_logs, get_health_status, get_process_detail, restart_process (error handling), stop_all_processes (error handling).

Config format:


Behavior:
- If true, restart process when it exits with non-zero code
- If false, leave crashed (current behavior)
- Log restart events
- Configurable max restarts? (or unlimited for now)

Default: false (to be decided after testing)

title: Generate OpenAPI 3.0 specification
description:
Generate OpenAPI 3.0 specification for the REST API.

DECISION NEEDED: Choose approach
1. **Manual YAML** (Recommended) - Full control, easier to review
2. **swaggo/swag** - Go annotations, auto-generated
3. **go-swagger** - Annotations + code generation

ENDPOINTS TO DOCUMENT:
- GET /health - System health and sources
- GET /logs - Query logs with filters (source, since, level, errors)
- GET /errors - Recent errors
- GET /processes - Process status

SPEC SHOULD INCLUDE:
- Request/response schemas
- Query parameters with types and examples
- Error responses (400, 500)
- Example requests/responses
- Server URL (http://localhost:9000)

OUTPUT: docs/openapi.yaml or api/openapi.json

ACCEPTANCE:
- Spec validates with OpenAPI 3.0 schema
- All endpoints documented
- Examples are accurate
- Can be imported into Postman/Insomnia

title: TUI: Fix newline rendering in log messages
description:
Log messages containing newlines (e.g., from progress bars, stack traces) are not rendered correctly in the TUI. Each log entry is formatted as a single line in renderLogs() (tui.go:194), which causes newlines to be collapsed or displayed incorrectly. This is particularly problematic for progress bars and multi-line output.

Fix: Split log messages on newlines and render each line separately, or use a viewport that handles multiline content properly.
close_reason: Fixed newline rendering in TUI log viewer. Messages with newlines (progress bars, stack traces) are now split and rendered on separate lines. Continuation lines are indented for clarity. Added comprehensive tests.

title: Define running-man.yml schema
description: Define Go structs for running-man.yml schema. Include fields for: processes ([]ProcessConfig), docker_compose (string), api_port (int), retention (duration), max_entries (int), max_bytes (int), shell (string). Create proper YAML tags and validation.
close_reason: Schema complete with validation and 96.2% test coverage

- Include surrounding log context (default 10 lines)

- Handle no errors case gracefully

Tool wraps existing /errors endpoint logic plus context

title: Implement get_health_status MCP tool
description:
Implement get_health_status MCP tool that provides system health information. Tool should:
- Return buffer statistics (total entries, bytes, oldest/newest entries)

- Include system uptime and memory usage if available
- Format as readable health report for AI agents

This wraps the existing /health endpoint logic with additional context.

close_reason: Exit code handling complete. Manager.ExitCodes() returns map of process names to codes, Manager.Wait() returns error if any fail, main.go prints non-zero codes. Test suite validates mixed exit codes.

title: Add list_processes MCP tool for intuitive process listing
description: Create a separate list_processes MCP tool that provides a simple list of all managed processes. While get_process_status can list processes with empty parameters, a dedicated list_processes tool is more intuitive for AI agents who just want to see what processes are available. The tool should return a simple list of process names with basic status, making it easier for agents to discover available processes before using other tools.
close_reason: get_process_status already provides process listing functionality when called with empty parameters {}. Documentation can be improved instead of adding duplicate tool.

title: Implement search_logs MCP tool
description:
Implement mcpSearchLogs() function:
- Define SearchLogsArgs struct with jsonschema tags
- Parse filters (source, since, level, contains, limit)
- Query buffer directly (no HTTP)
- Format results as readable text
- Handle edge cases (no results, invalid params)

Tool wraps existing /logs endpoint logic
close_reason: Implemented search_logs MCP tool with parameter validation, DoS protection, and readable output formatting. Handles filters for source, time window, log level, and text search. Includes input validation and edge case handling.

title: Log Parsing Engine: Python tracebacks
description: Python traceback detection and grouping with regex patterns and multi-line error aggregation
close_reason: Implemented all parsers: JSON (with flexible field extraction), Python traceback (multi-line, stateful), plain text (heuristic level detection). Multi-parser with precedence: JSON > Python > plain text. All 20+ tests passing.

title: Add GET /processes endpoint

close_reason: GET /processes endpoint implemented and tested. Returns list of all processes with status info.

title: Inject OTEL environment variables to processes
description:
Automatically inject OTEL environment variables when starting managed processes.

Requirements:
- Inject OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:4318

- Inject OTEL_PROPAGATORS=tracecontext,baggage (full context)

- Works with both process and Docker Compose sources

title: TUI: Add scroll controls
description:
Add keyboard-based scrolling to TUI log viewer:

REQUIREMENTS:
- Up/Down arrows: Scroll one line at a time  
- PgUp/PgDn: Scroll one page at a time
- Home: Jump to oldest log
- End: Jump to newest log (default view)
- Maintain scroll position until user presses End or new logs arrive

IMPLEMENTATION:

- Update View() to render from offset
- Update Update() to handle key events
- Auto-scroll to bottom when at end position

ACCEPTANCE:
- User can navigate through full log history
- Scrolling is smooth and responsive
- Auto-follow mode works when at bottom
close_reason: Implemented scroll controls with full keyboard navigation

title: Support environment variable substitution in config
description: Allow ${VAR} syntax in config file values for flexibility. Example: command: 'npm run ${BUILD_MODE}' where BUILD_MODE can be set in environment.
close_reason: Implemented environment variable substitution using os.ExpandEnv(). Supports  and  syntax in command, args, shell, and docker_compose fields. Undefined variables expand to empty string. Added comprehensive tests and updated example config.

title: Phase 2: Multi-Source Capture
description: Multi-source log capture for real-world dev stacks. Success criteria: handle 5+ processes simultaneously, capture Docker container logs, source filtering works correctly, survives container restarts with <5s lag. This epic validates that all Phase 2 features work together in production-like scenarios.
close_reason: Phase 2 complete. Multi-source capture working, Docker integration fixed, config system functional. TUI issues filed separately (the_running_man-fql, the_running_man-vby).

title: TUI: Logs disappear after 5 minutes
description:
The TUI only fetches logs from the last 5 minutes (tui.go:237 uses 'since=5m' parameter). This causes older logs to disappear from the TUI view, even though they're still in the ring buffer.

Impact: Users lose visibility into historical logs during a session, making debugging difficult.

Expected: TUI should show all logs from the current session (up to retention limit), not just the last 5 minutes.

Fix options:
1. Remove the 'since=5m' parameter to show all available logs
2. Make the time window configurable
3. Add pagination/scrolling to handle large log volumes
close_reason: Removed hardcoded 5-minute time window from TUI log fetching. Now shows all logs from the ring buffer (up to 30min/50MB retention limit).

title: Log Parsing Engine: JSON & plain text
description: JSON log parsing with field extraction, plain text with heuristic level detection (ERROR, WARN, INFO)
close_reason: Implemented all parsers: JSON (with flexible field extraction), Python traceback (multi-line, stateful), plain text (heuristic level detection). Multi-parser with precedence: JSON > Python > plain text. All 20+ tests passing.

title: Refactor wrapper for multi-process support
description: Modify wrapper.Wrapper to manage multiple child processes, each with unique source name
close_reason: Implemented Manager for multi-process orchestration. Fixed all critical concurrency issues: goroutine leak in signal handler, race condition in Restart(), timer leak in Stop(). All tests pass including race detector.

title: TUI: Add cursor movement in search mode
description:
Add proper text input in search mode using Bubble Tea's textinput component.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Use github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput for full readline-like functionality
- Left/right arrows move cursor
- Home/End jump to start/end
- Backspace deletes before cursor
- Character insertion at cursor position
- Visual cursor indicator (handled by textinput)

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add textinput.Model field to model
- Initialize textinput with placeholder, focus, etc.
- In search mode, delegate to textinput.Update() for key handling
- Render textinput.View() in search bar
- Extract textinput.Value() when exiting search mode

DEPENDENCIES:
- github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput

NOTES:
- Bubble Tea's textinput provides all readline functionality out of the box
- Much simpler than implementing cursor tracking ourselves
close_reason: Already implemented: searchInput textinput.Model is used with textinput.Update() for full readline-like cursor movement (left/right, home/end, backspace, insertion at cursor)

title: Phase 4: OTEL Tracing & Visualization
description:
Add OpenTelemetry tracing support to Running Man for full-stack observability.

Goals:
- OTLP HTTP receiver (:4318) for trace ingestion
- Environment variable injection for auto-instrumentation
- Trace-log correlation via trace_id
- Basic TUI visualization
- MCP tools for trace querying
- Optional web UI for complex trace viewing

Scope:
- Direct OTLP receiver (no separate collector)
- Full context propagation support
- Integrated buffer with logs
- No sampling initially (local dev scale)
- HTTP OTLP only initially

Success criteria:
- Python/Node apps can send traces automatically
- Traces correlated with logs in TUI
- MCP tools for trace debugging
- Documentation for setup and usage

- New trace tab in TUI (press 't')
- List recent traces with summary (duration, status, service)
- Select trace to view spans tree
- Show correlated logs with trace
- Basic ASCII visualization of span hierarchy

Implementation:
- Extend TUI model with trace view state
- New trace list and detail views
- ASCII tree rendering for spans
- Integration with trace storage
- Navigation between trace list and detail
